rules.title: REDD forest land definition
rules.text: The project area must be on land that qualifies as forest and meets the host-country forest definition for a minimum of 10 years prior to the project start date. Mangrove forests are exempt from tree height requirements.
rules.logic: Project land must meet forest definition threshold for 10+ years. Mangroves exempt from height.

rules.title: REDD baseline deforestation category
rules.text: Baseline deforestation must fall into one of two categories: (a) unplanned deforestation (AUDef) where agents have no legal right to deforest, or (b) planned deforestation (APDef) where conversion is legally permitted.
rules.logic: Binary classification: unplanned (no legal right) vs planned (legally permitted conversion). Determines which BL module applies.

rules.title: AUDef agent criteria
rules.text: For unplanned deforestation, baseline agents must: (a) clear land for harvesting, settlements, crop production, ranching, or aquaculture (not large-scale industrial); (b) have no documented legal right to deforest; (c) have deforested in the historical reference period.
rules.logic: Three criteria must ALL be met. Excludes large-scale industrial agriculture/aquaculture.

rules.title: APDef legal authorization
rules.text: For planned deforestation, the conversion of forest lands to non-forest must be legally authorized and documented.
rules.logic: Must have legal documentation authorizing conversion.

rules.title: WRC water table prohibition
rules.text: WRC activities are not eligible if they lower the water table, unless the project converts open water to tidal wetlands or improves hydrological connection to impounded waters.
rules.logic: Water table lowering is prohibited except for two specific exceptions.

rules.title: WRC organic soil burning prohibition
rules.text: WRC project activities must not include burning of organic soil.
rules.logic: Absolute prohibition on organic soil burning as a project activity.

rules.title: WRC nitrogen fertilizer prohibition
rules.text: Nitrogen fertilizers (chemical fertilizer or manure) must not be applied in the project area during the crediting period.
rules.logic: No nitrogen fertilizer application allowed during entire crediting period.

rules.title: WRC hydrological connectivity check
rules.text: Hydrological connectivity of the project area with adjacent areas must not lead to a significant increase in GHG emissions outside the project area.
rules.logic: Spillover emissions from hydrological changes must be assessed and not be significant.

rules.title: RWE prior land use requirement
rules.text: For RWE projects, prior to the project start date the area must meet one of: (a) free of displacable land use; (b) under land use where displacement is prohibited by enforced law; or (c) under land use that continues at similar or greater level during crediting.
rules.logic: Three alternative conditions. Must demonstrate one with verifiable evidence.

rules.title: Peatland VCS definition
rules.text: Peatland rewetting (RDP) and conservation (CUPP) activities must occur on areas meeting the VCS definition for peatland (organic soil with sufficient depth and percentage of organic carbon per IPCC Wetlands supplement thresholds).
rules.logic: Must meet VCS peatland definition based on IPCC organic soil thresholds.

rules.title: Tidal wetland restoration activities
rules.text: Tidal wetland restoration must include one or more of: creating/restoring hydrology, altering sediment supply, changing salinity, managing vegetation, prescribed burning of herbaceous biomass.
rules.logic: Must include at least one defined restoration activity type.

rules.title: CIW tidal wetland conservation activities
rules.text: CIW activities conserving tidal wetlands must include: protecting at-risk wetlands, improving water management, maintaining water quality, recharging sediment, creating accommodation space for wetland migration.
rules.logic: Must include at least one defined conservation activity type.

rules.title: AUWD agent criteria
rules.text: For avoiding unplanned wetland degradation, baseline agents must: (a) cause alteration in hydrology or loss of SOC; (b) have no documented legal right to degrade; (c) have degraded wetlands in the historical reference period.
rules.logic: Parallel to AUDef criteria but for wetland degradation.

rules.title: IFM and ARR exclusion
rules.text: Improved forest management (IFM) and Afforestation, Reforestation, and Revegetation (ARR) are not covered by this methodology. Where WRC projects implement ARR, use VM0047 for biomass and WRC modules for other pools.
rules.logic: IFM and ARR explicitly excluded. ARR in wetlands requires VM0047 + WRC modules.

rules.title: Leakage prevention activity restrictions
rules.text: REDD activities are not eligible if leakage prevention includes flooding agricultural lands (e.g., rice paddies) or intensifying livestock production through feed-lots or manure lagoons.
rules.logic: Two specific leakage prevention activities are explicitly prohibited.

rules.title: Geographic boundary definition
rules.text: Spatial boundaries must be clearly defined with: name of project area, unique ID per parcel, maps per VCS Standard, geographic coordinates, total area per stratum, landholder/user rights.
rules.logic: Six required data elements for each discrete land parcel.

rules.title: No spatial overlap between baselines
rules.text: Where multiple baselines exist (planned, unplanned, forest degradation, degraded land), there must be no spatial overlap between areas appropriate to each baseline. Combined REDD+WRC is the only exception.
rules.logic: Strict no-overlap rule. REDD+WRC combination is the sole exception.

rules.title: Exclusion of land in other GHG programs
rules.text: All land registered under any other GHG program must be transparently reported and excluded from the project area. Exclusion must be monitored over time and reported in monitoring reports.
rules.logic: Double-counting prevention. Must track and report exclusions.

rules.title: REDD reference region definition
rules.text: For unplanned deforestation, a Reference Region for Deforestation (RRD) must be defined within the same jurisdiction as the project area with similar characteristics. Refer to Module BL-UP.
rules.logic: RRD required for baseline modeling. Must be in same jurisdiction with similar characteristics.

rules.title: REDD proxy area definition
rules.text: For planned deforestation, proxy area(s) must be defined. Refer to Module BL-PL for detailed boundary procedures.
rules.logic: Proxy areas required for planned deforestation baseline.

rules.title: Stratification requirement
rules.text: The project area must be stratified into strata based on activity type, land use, and carbon pool characteristics. Strata must be defined and mapped at validation. Use Module X-STR.
rules.logic: Stratification mandatory. Different activity types must be in separate strata.

rules.title: Carbon pool selection per activity type
rules.text: For each activity type, identify which carbon pools (above-ground biomass, below-ground biomass, dead wood, litter, SOC, harvested wood products) are included or excluded with justification. Use T-SIG to test significance of excluded pools.
rules.logic: Pool inclusion/exclusion must be justified. If included in baseline, must also be in project and leakage.

rules.title: REDD mandatory carbon pools
rules.text: For REDD projects, above-ground biomass is mandatory. Harvested wood products and dead wood must be included when they increase more or decrease less in baseline than project. All other pools are optional.
rules.logic: AGB always mandatory. HWP and dead wood conditionally mandatory based on baseline/project comparison.

rules.title: WRC carbon pools
rules.text: For WRC projects: above-ground tree biomass, below-ground biomass, litter, dead wood, and wood products are excluded (covered under REDD). Soil organic carbon is included via Modules BL-PEAT/M-PEAT or BL-TW/M-TW.
rules.logic: WRC focuses on SOC pool. Biomass pools excluded unless ARR applies.

rules.title: REDD GHG source identification
rules.text: For REDD projects, account for CO2, CH4, N2O from: baseline deforestation/degradation, project burning of woody biomass, project fossil fuel combustion, baseline/project fertilizer use. Justify each inclusion/exclusion.
rules.logic: GHG sources must be identified per Table 7 with inclusion/exclusion justification.

rules.title: WRC GHG source identification
rules.text: For WRC projects: account for CO2 and CH4 from peat oxidation/tidal wetland mineral soil, CO2/CH4/N2O from biomass/peat combustion, CO2/CH4/N2O from fossil fuel combustion. Per Tables 8 and 9.
rules.logic: GHG sources per Tables 8-9. Mandatory for CO2 from SOC, CH4 from SOC, combustion gases.

rules.title: Consistency rule: baseline-project source matching
rules.text: If a GHG source is included in baseline emissions estimation, it must also be included in project and leakage emissions estimation.
rules.logic: Symmetric accounting: baseline inclusion implies project inclusion.

rules.title: Historical reference period
rules.text: The historical reference period is the fixed time period for analyzing historical deforestation/degradation in the reference region. Duration per VCS Methodology Requirements. Ends at crediting period start (validation) or last monitoring period end (reassessment).
rules.logic: Fixed period for baseline modeling. Duration per VCS Standard.

rules.title: Crediting period duration
rules.text: AFOLU project crediting period must be between 20 and 100 years. Duration must be reported in PD.
rules.logic: 20-100 year range. Must be specified in PD.

rules.title: WRC 100-year SOC difference requirement
rules.text: The maximum eligible GHG reductions in WRC activities is limited to the difference between remaining SOC stock in project and baseline scenarios after 100 years. If no significant difference at 100 years, the area is not eligible. Assessed ex-ante using conservative parameters via Module X-STR.
rules.logic: 100-year SOC horizon test. Must show significant difference. Assessed ex-ante conservatively.

rules.title: Tidal wetland sea-level rise boundary
rules.text: WRC activities in tidal zones must account for effects of sea-level rise on project boundaries. Procedures in Module X-STR.
rules.logic: Sea-level rise must be factored into boundary delineation for tidal projects.

rules.title: Baseline scenario determination via VT0001
rules.text: The most plausible baseline scenario must be determined using VT0001 and the stepwise approach in Section 6. Must be consistent with conditions prior to project start date.
rules.logic: VT0001 mandatory. Stepwise approach: list alternatives, barrier/investment analysis, select most plausible.

rules.title: Minimum alternative scenario list
rules.text: Plausible alternative scenarios must include at minimum: (1) continuation of pre-project land use; (2) project activity without VCS registration; (3) similar activities on part of project land.
rules.logic: Three mandatory alternatives unless demonstrated not credible or non-compliant.

rules.title: Barrier analysis baseline selection
rules.text: Where VT0001 barrier analysis is used: apply decision tree to scenarios not prevented by barriers. If barrier analysis alone cannot determine baseline, supplement with investment analysis (VT0001 Option II) or qualitative GHG estimation using IPCC defaults.
rules.logic: Two-step: barrier analysis first, then investment analysis or qualitative GHG estimation as fallback.

rules.title: Investment analysis baseline selection
rules.text: Where VT0001 investment analysis is used: Option I selects lowest cost scenario (no revenues); Option II selects most financially attractive; Option III selects scenario meeting benchmark or pre-project land use if none meet benchmark.
rules.logic: Three options with different selection criteria. Option I is cost-only, Option II is IRR/NPV, Option III is benchmark-based.

rules.title: REDD baseline modules
rules.text: For REDD: baseline estimation uses Module BL-PL for planned deforestation/degradation and Module BL-UP for unplanned deforestation.
rules.logic: Module selection based on deforestation category.

rules.title: WRC baseline modules
rules.text: For WRC: baseline SOC emissions estimated using Module BL-PEAT (peatland) or BL-TW (tidal wetland). Stand-alone CIW uses BL-TW or BL-PEAT with BL-UP or BL-PL. Stand-alone RWE uses BL-PEAT or BL-TW for SOC pool.
rules.logic: Module selection depends on substrate (peat vs tidal) and activity type (CIW vs RWE vs combined).

rules.title: Baseline reassessment frequency
rules.text: The project baseline must be revised at the frequency set out in the latest VCS Standard. Starting point for revision is projected forest cover at end of baseline period. Projections subject to independent validation.
rules.logic: Periodic reassessment per VCS Standard. Must capture changes in deforestation drivers/behavior.

rules.title: JNR data use
rules.text: Where data from a jurisdictional baseline meeting VCS JNR Requirements is available, it may be used under this methodology where conservative, even if data accuracy is less stringent than required by this methodology.
rules.logic: JNR data is acceptable as a conservative source even if less accurate.

rules.title: VT0001 additionality requirement
rules.text: VT0001 Tool for Demonstration and Assessment of Additionality in VCS AFOLU Project Activities must be used for all project activities other than tidal wetland conservation and restoration.
rules.logic: VT0001 mandatory for all non-tidal-wetland activities.

rules.title: Tidal wetland additionality method
rules.text: For tidal wetland conservation and restoration activities, use ADD-AM (Demonstration of Additionality of Tidal Wetland Restoration and Conservation Project Activities) instead of VT0001.
rules.logic: Activity method (ADD-AM) replaces project method (VT0001) for tidal wetlands only.

rules.title: REDD net emission reduction equation
rules.text: NER_REDD = GHG_BSL-REDD - GHG_WPS-REDD - GHG_LK-REDD (Equation 1). Total net emission reductions equals baseline emissions minus project emissions minus leakage.
rules.logic: Standard REDD equation. Three components summed over monitoring period.

rules.title: WRC net emission reduction equation
rules.text: NER_WRC = GHG_BSL-WRC - GHG_WPS-WRC - GHG_LK-WRC (Equation 5). Same structure as REDD but for wetland components.
rules.logic: Parallel to REDD equation. Components use peat/tidal wetland modules.

rules.title: REDD leakage components
rules.text: REDD leakage: LK-REDD = LK-AS-planned + LK-AS-unplanned + LK-ME (Equation 4). Activity shifting (planned and unplanned) plus market effects.
rules.logic: Three leakage components. Activity shifting via LK-ASP and LK-ASU modules. Market effects via LK-ME.

rules.title: WRC leakage components
rules.text: WRC leakage: LK-WRC = LK-AS-planned + LK-AS-unplanned + LK-ECO (Equation 11). Includes ecological leakage via LK-ECO module.
rules.logic: Three components including ecological leakage specific to wetlands.

rules.title: Buffer pool contribution calculation
rules.text: Buffer contribution calculated separately for planned (Eq 13), unplanned (Eq 14), and WRC (Eq 15) using T-BAR risk classification percentage. Formula: Buffer_total = Buffer_planned + Buffer_unplanned + Buffer_WRC (Eq 12).
rules.logic: Three buffer components. Each = (baseline - project) × Buffer%. Buffer% from T-BAR.

rules.title: Uncertainty adjustment
rules.text: Net GHG reductions adjusted using Module X-UNC. Allowable uncertainty is +/-15% of NER_REDD+ at 90% confidence. Where exceeded: Adjusted_NER = NER × (100 - NER_ERROR + 15). Below 15%, no adjustment needed.
rules.logic: 15% threshold at 90% CI. Excess deducted proportionally. Below threshold, no deduction.

rules.title: VCU calculation
rules.text: VCU_t = (Adjusted_NER_REDD+,t2 - Adjusted_NER_REDD+,t1) - Buffer_total (Equation 16). Credits for monitoring period = cumulative adjusted reductions minus buffer.
rules.logic: Period credits = incremental adjusted NER minus buffer withholding.

rules.title: Carbon pool modules for REDD
rules.text: REDD carbon stock estimation uses: VMD0001 (CP-AB: above/below-ground biomass), VMD0002 (CP-D: dead wood), VMD0003 (CP-L: litter), VMD0004 (CP-S: soil organic carbon), VMD0005 (CP-W: harvested wood products).
rules.logic: Five carbon pool modules. Each optional except AG biomass per R-2-0008.

rules.title: Project emissions modules
rules.text: Project emissions estimated using: Module M-REDD for REDD activities, Module M-PEAT for peatland WRC, Module M-TW for tidal wetland WRC. E-BPB for biomass/peat burning, E-FFC for fossil fuel combustion, E-NA for N2O from fertilizer.
rules.logic: Module selection by activity type and emission source.

rules.title: Monitoring plan four tasks
rules.text: The monitoring plan must address: (1) monitoring project implementation, (2) monitoring actual carbon stock changes and GHG emissions, (3) monitoring leakage carbon stock changes, (4) ex post estimation of net changes.
rules.logic: Four mandatory monitoring tasks. Each requires technical description, data list, procedures, QA/QC, archiving, responsibilities.

rules.title: Monitoring plan content requirements
rules.text: For each monitoring task: technical description, data to collect, data collection procedures, QA/QC procedures, data archiving, organizational responsibilities.
rules.logic: Six required content elements per task.

rules.title: Geographic position recording
rules.text: Geographic position of project boundary must be recorded for all land areas using GPS field survey or georeferenced spatial data. Also applies to strata and peatland fire strata.
rules.logic: GPS or georeferenced data mandatory. Strata included.

rules.title: SOP and QA/QC requirements
rules.text: Standard operating procedures and QA/QC required: SOPs from national monitoring or IPCC guidance, comprehensive field documentation, training records, check cruise protocols, outlier assessment, data archiving for project lifetime.
rules.logic: Six minimum SOP/QA/QC elements.

rules.title: REDD forest cover monitoring
rules.text: Changes in forest cover in the RRD must be monitored at minimum every 10 years per Module M-REDD and BL-UP. Spatial variable datasets for deforestation location modeling must be monitored at reassessment.
rules.logic: 10-year minimum frequency. Spatial variables must be refreshed.

rules.title: WRC compliance monitoring
rules.text: WRC projects must monitor: water table not lowered, peatland fires do not occur (or account via Fire Reduction Premium), nitrogen fertilizers not used within project area.
rules.logic: Three ongoing compliance checks. Fire triggers premium cancellation.

rules.title: Expert judgment documentation
rules.text: When expert judgment is used for data selection or interpretation, follow IPCC 2006 guidance Chapter 2 Section 2.2 and Annex 2A.1. Document rationale for selecting particular values from ranges.
rules.logic: IPCC 2006 expert judgment protocol mandatory. Rationale must be documented.

rules.title: Uncertainty reduction requirements
rules.text: Key parameters influencing accuracy must be identified. Local values obtained from peer-reviewed literature, IPCC defaults (checked against local data), or expert opinion with documented rationale.
rules.logic: Three data source tiers: literature > IPCC defaults > expert opinion.
